known_facts: The product monitors warehouse query cost spikes.
The current hero is abstract and lacks product evidence.
The page currently shows three CTAs in the first viewport.
The tone must stay plain and technical.
explicit_constraints: Secondary CTA must be 'See query sample'.
Use the existing 8px spacing scale and 12px radius.
labeled_assumptions: Assumption: both desktop and mobile web matter.
Assumption: one product screenshot or annotated sample query can be shown above the fold.

design_principles: Explain the product in concrete warehouse-cost terms before describing platform breadth.
Keep one dominant action in the first viewport and demote the lower-risk exploration path.
Use proof early: show sample query output, detected spike, and cost delta instead of abstract decoration.

layout_plan.primary_goal: Make the offer legible in one scan and route serious buyers to the demo CTA.
section_order: Hero with product sentence, proof screenshot, and one primary CTA
Three concrete use cases tied to cost spikes, regressions, and owner handoff
Short proof strip with customer context or sample savings format
FAQ handling setup complexity and warehouse support
hierarchy_notes: Lead with a headline about catching expensive queries before spend compounds.
Place the screenshot next to the headline on desktop and under it on mobile.
Keep the secondary CTA as a text link or low-emphasis button near the primary CTA.
responsive_strategy: Collapse the hero to a single column under tablet width.
Turn multi-column proof cards into stacked cards with unchanged reading order.

components.name: Hero CTA group
components.purpose: Route buyers to the main next step without splitting attention.
accessibility_notes: Primary and secondary actions need distinct accessible names.
Focus ring must remain visible against the neutral background.
implementation_notes: Use one high-emphasis button for the demo and a low-emphasis button or text link for the sample query.
Do not add a third CTA in the hero.

components.name: Proof screenshot block
components.purpose: Show what the product detects and why it matters.
accessibility_notes: Provide concise alt text that describes the highlighted cost spike.
Do not rely on color alone to indicate the spike.
implementation_notes: Use a static screenshot or annotated illustration with visible labels.
Reserve space to avoid layout shift.

content_guidance.headline: Catch expensive warehouse queries before they become a monthly surprise.
microcopy_rules: Name the warehouse problem before mentioning platform scope.
Use specific actions like detect, compare, and notify instead of hype verbs.
Keep feature labels tied to cost, ownership, and regression review.

semantic_html: Use a single h1 in the hero and landmark regions for main, nav, and footer.
Structure use cases as a list rather than decorative cards with unnamed headings.
keyboard_and_focus: Ensure the hero CTA group is reachable in a logical order.
Retain visible focus treatment on all interactive proof links.
screen_reader_notes: Give the screenshot block a concise text description.
Expose proof-strip headings in a meaningful reading order.
motion_and_color: Avoid ambient animation in the hero.
Use icon shape or text labels, not color alone, to distinguish severity.
performance_actions: Compress the hero image and reserve its layout space.
Avoid a large client-rendered carousel in the proof section.

risks.issue: The current message is too abstract.
risks.reason: Buyers cannot tell what the product detects in the first viewport.
Three first-view actions weaken the main conversion path.

next_actions: Rewrite the hero around warehouse query cost spikes and add a single proof screenshot.
Reduce the hero to one primary CTA and one lower-emphasis secondary path.
Move proof and pricing above late-scroll FAQ content.
